roadmap.milestone_count_rationale: 6 milestones: Rajesh is a beginner with a 64-hour budget and a goal of an internship within 3 months. This requires a structured, progressive path with smaller, safer steps to build confidence and capabilities incrementally, aligning with a self-efficacy of 0.5. Each milestone represents a distinct, market-recognized identity.

vision_profile.current_state: A student learning foundational backend development concepts, aiming to apply knowledge in a professional setting.
vision_profile.main_blocker: Lack of structured, industry-aligned experience and a clear path to translate theoretical knowledge into practical, job-ready skills.
vision_profile.top_motivation: To secure a backend development internship within the next three months and gain real-world experience.

milestones.label: M01
milestones.o: Rajesh can understand core backend principles, write clean and testable server-side code, and contribute to basic API development.
milestones.module_count_rationale: 4 modules because this milestone covers foundational programming, core server-side concepts, data persistence, and basic deployment, providing a comprehensive entry point to backend development.
milestones.identity_statement: You are becoming a Backend Foundations Intern, ready to build the core logic that powers applications.

project.title: Build a Personal Portfolio API
project.description: Develop a simple RESTful API for a personal portfolio or blog. This project will focus on clean architecture, basic data persistence, and deploying your first backend service.
project.deliverable: A deployed API with documentation (e.g., Swagger/Postman collection) and a GitHub repository demonstrating clean code and database interaction.
seeded_errors: The API's database connection string is hardcoded and exposed in a public repository, requiring the learner to implement environment variable management.

modules.title: Foundational Programming for Backend
modules.skill_count_rationale: 5 skills are essential to establish a strong programming base for backend development, covering language fundamentals, data handling, and structured coding.

skills.title: Python Fundamentals for Backend
skills.why_this_skill: Python is a versatile language widely used in backend development, offering simplicity and powerful libraries for web services and data processing.

scenario.title: Debugging a Python Script

skills.title: Essential Data Structures
skills.why_this_skill: Understanding data structures is crucial for efficient data handling and algorithm design, directly impacting backend service performance.

scenario.title: Optimizing Data Storage for User Profiles

skills.title: Basic Algorithms & Problem Solving
skills.why_this_skill: Algorithms are fundamental for writing efficient and scalable backend logic, a key skill for technical interviews and production systems.

scenario.title: Optimizing User Search Functionality

skills.title: Object-Oriented Programming (OOP)
skills.why_this_skill: OOP principles are fundamental for writing modular, reusable, and maintainable backend code, essential for collaborative projects.

scenario.title: Refactoring a Monolithic Service with OOP

skills.title: Robust Error Handling & Logging
skills.why_this_skill: Proper error handling and logging are critical for building resilient backend services and effectively debugging issues in production.

scenario.title: Diagnosing a Production Error from Logs

modules.title: Core Server-Side Concepts & API Basics
modules.skill_count_rationale: 5 skills are necessary to grasp the fundamental communication protocols and architectural styles for building web services.

skills.title: HTTP/HTTPS Protocol Fundamentals
skills.why_this_skill: Understanding HTTP/HTTPS is the bedrock of web communication, essential for any backend developer to build and secure web services.

scenario.title: Debugging a Failed API Request

skills.title: RESTful API Design Principles
skills.why_this_skill: REST is the most common architectural style for web APIs; mastering its principles is essential for designing interoperable and scalable services.

scenario.title: Designing a RESTful Endpoint for User Management

skills.title: Implementing API Endpoints
skills.why_this_skill: Directly implementing API endpoints is the core task of a backend developer, translating design into functional code.

scenario.title: Refactoring an Inconsistent API Endpoint

skills.title: Request & Response Handling
skills.why_this_skill: Efficiently processing incoming requests and crafting appropriate responses is central to any functional backend service.

scenario.title: Handling Malformed API Requests Gracefully

skills.title: Middleware & Request Processing
skills.why_this_skill: Middleware allows for modular and reusable logic execution before or after API endpoint handling, crucial for tasks like authentication or logging.

scenario.title: Implementing a Rate Limiting Middleware

science.desc: A critical API endpoint is returning incorrect data due to an issue in request parsing. Diagnose the problem and implement a fix.

modules.title: Data Persistence & Basic Database Interaction
modules.skill_count_rationale: 5 skills are crucial for understanding how to store, retrieve, and manage data, which is the backbone of almost all backend applications.

skills.title: Introduction to Relational Databases (SQL)
skills.why_this_skill: SQL databases are foundational in many backend systems; understanding them is key for data storage and retrieval.

scenario.title: Troubleshooting a Slow SQL Query

skills.title: NoSQL Database Concepts
skills.why_this_skill: Exposure to NoSQL databases broadens a backend developer's toolkit, allowing for flexible data modeling in various use cases.

scenario.title: Choosing a Database for a New Feature

skills.title: ORM/ODM Basics for Database Interaction
skills.why_this_skill: ORMs/ODMs simplify database interactions, allowing developers to work with database records as objects, improving productivity and code readability.

scenario.title: Debugging an ORM Query Performance Issue

skills.title: Basic Data Modeling & Schema Design
skills.why_this_skill: Effective data modeling is crucial for designing efficient and scalable databases, directly impacting application performance and maintainability.

scenario.title: Redesigning a Database Schema for Scalability

skills.title: Implementing CRUD Operations
skills.why_this_skill: CRUD (Create, Read, Update, Delete) operations are the fundamental interactions with any database, forming the core of most backend services.

scenario.title: Ensuring Data Integrity During Updates

modules.title: Version Control & Basic Deployment
modules.skill_count_rationale: 5 skills are critical for collaborative development, managing code changes, and making backend services accessible to users.

skills.title: Git Fundamentals for Collaboration
skills.why_this_skill: Git is the industry standard for version control, essential for tracking code changes and collaborating effectively in a team.

scenario.title: Recovering from a Bad Git Commit

skills.title: GitHub Workflow & Pull Requests
skills.why_this_skill: GitHub is the leading platform for code hosting and collaboration; mastering its workflow is essential for open-source contributions and team projects.

scenario.title: Collaborating on a Feature Branch with GitHub

skills.title: Basic Cloud Deployment (PaaS)
skills.why_this_skill: Deploying applications to the cloud is a fundamental skill for making backend services accessible to users globally, often starting with Platform as a Service (PaaS).
lessons: Deploying to a PaaS (e.g., Heroku/Render concepts)

scenario.title: Troubleshooting a Failed PaaS Deployment

skills.title: Introduction to Containerization (Docker)
skills.why_this_skill: Containerization with Docker provides consistent environments for development and deployment, simplifying dependency management and scaling.

scenario.title: Containerizing a Simple Backend Application

skills.title: Managing Configuration with Environment Variables
skills.why_this_skill: Securely managing sensitive information and configurations using environment variables is a best practice for production-ready applications.

scenario.title: Securing API Keys in a Deployed Application

science.desc: Explain the core components of a RESTful API and how you would deploy a simple backend service to a cloud platform.

milestones.label: M02
milestones.o: Rajesh can design, implement, and secure robust API endpoints, including user authentication and data validation, and integrate with external services.
milestones.module_count_rationale: 4 modules are needed to cover advanced API design, security, authentication, data validation, and external integrations, which are critical for building production-ready APIs.
milestones.identity_statement: You are evolving into an API Developer Intern, building secure and functional interfaces for applications.

project.title: Secure Task Management API
project.description: Develop a secure API for a task management application. This project will include user authentication (e.g., JWT), robust data validation, and proper error handling for API requests.
project.deliverable: A deployed API with user registration/login, task CRUD operations, input validation, and API documentation. Include a security report outlining implemented measures.
seeded_errors: The API allows direct access to user data without proper authorization checks, requiring the learner to implement role-based access control.

modules.title: Advanced API Design & Security
modules.skill_count_rationale: 5 skills are essential for designing well-structured, maintainable, and secure APIs that adhere to industry best practices.

skills.title: API Versioning Strategies
skills.why_this_skill: Versioning ensures backward compatibility and allows for evolving APIs without breaking existing clients, a critical aspect of long-term API maintenance.

scenario.title: Migrating an API to a New Version

skills.title: API Documentation (Swagger/OpenAPI)
skills.why_this_skill: Clear API documentation is vital for developers consuming your API, improving usability and reducing integration time.

scenario.title: Documenting a Complex API Endpoint

skills.title: API Security Best Practices
skills.why_this_skill: Securing APIs against common vulnerabilities is paramount to protect data and maintain user trust.

scenario.title: Preventing SQL Injection in an API

skills.title: CORS & Cross-Origin Security
skills.why_this_skill: Understanding and correctly configuring CORS is crucial for allowing web applications to securely interact with your backend API.

scenario.title: Resolving a CORS Policy Error

skills.title: Introduction to API Gateways
skills.why_this_skill: API Gateways are crucial for managing, securing, and routing requests to multiple backend services, especially in microservices architectures.

scenario.title: Designing an API Gateway for Multiple Services

modules.title: Authentication & Authorization
modules.skill_count_rationale: 5 skills are critical for implementing secure user access control, protecting sensitive data, and managing user permissions.

skills.title: User Authentication Methods
skills.why_this_skill: Implementing secure user authentication is fundamental for any application that requires user accounts and personalized experiences.

scenario.title: Implementing Secure User Registration

skills.title: JWT (JSON Web Token) Authentication
skills.why_this_skill: JWTs are a popular, stateless authentication method widely used in modern web applications and microservices.

scenario.title: Securing API Endpoints with JWT

skills.title: OAuth 2.0 & OpenID Connect (Concepts)
skills.why_this_skill: Understanding OAuth and OpenID Connect is crucial for integrating third-party authentication (e.g., 'Login with Google') and building secure delegated authorization.

scenario.title: Integrating Third-Party Login with OAuth

skills.title: User Authorization & Access Control
skills.why_this_skill: Authorization ensures users only access resources they are permitted to, a critical component of secure multi-user applications.

scenario.title: Restricting Access to Admin-Only Endpoints

skills.title: Secure Password Storage & Management
skills.why_this_skill: Properly storing and managing user passwords is a non-negotiable security requirement for any application handling user data.

scenario.title: Implementing a Secure Password Reset Mechanism

science.desc: A new feature requires different access levels for users (admin, editor, viewer). Design and implement an authorization system to enforce these roles.

modules.title: Data Validation & Error Handling
modules.skill_count_rationale: 5 skills are crucial for ensuring data integrity, providing clear feedback to API consumers, and building resilient backend services.

skills.title: API Input Validation
skills.why_this_skill: Validating all incoming API input is a primary defense against malicious data and ensures data integrity within your application.

scenario.title: Validating User Registration Data

skills.title: Custom Error Responses & Status Codes
skills.why_this_skill: Providing clear, consistent, and informative error responses improves the developer experience for API consumers and aids debugging.

scenario.title: Standardizing Error Responses Across API

skills.title: Database Transactions & Atomicity
skills.why_this_skill: Transactions ensure data consistency and integrity, especially for complex operations involving multiple database changes.

scenario.title: Ensuring Atomicity in a Payment Transaction

skills.title: Introduction to Unit Testing
skills.why_this_skill: Unit testing is fundamental for verifying the correctness of individual code components, reducing bugs and improving code quality.

scenario.title: Writing Unit Tests for a Utility Function

skills.title: Introduction to Integration Testing
skills.why_this_skill: Integration testing verifies that different parts of your backend system work together correctly, catching issues that unit tests might miss.

scenario.title: Testing an API with Database Interaction

modules.title: External Service Integration
modules.skill_count_rationale: 5 skills are necessary for connecting backend services with external APIs, handling data exchange, and managing dependencies.

skills.title: Consuming External REST APIs
skills.why_this_skill: Integrating with third-party services is a common backend task; mastering API consumption is essential for building feature-rich applications.

scenario.title: Integrating with a Weather API

skills.title: Webhooks & Event-Driven Communication
skills.why_this_skill: Webhooks enable real-time communication between services, crucial for event-driven architectures and immediate notifications.

scenario.title: Receiving Notifications from a Payment Gateway

skills.title: Integrating with SDKs & Libraries
skills.why_this_skill: Using SDKs simplifies integration with complex services, abstracting away low-level API calls and accelerating development.

scenario.title: Integrating a Cloud Storage SDK

skills.title: Data Serialization & Deserialization
skills.why_this_skill: Efficiently converting data between different formats (e.g., JSON, XML) is fundamental for inter-service communication and data storage.

scenario.title: Handling Inconsistent JSON Payloads

skills.title: API Rate Limiting & Quotas
skills.why_this_skill: Implementing rate limiting protects your API from abuse, ensures fair usage, and maintains service availability under high load.

scenario.title: Preventing API Abuse with Rate Limiting

science.desc: Describe how you would design a secure API for a mobile application, including authentication, authorization, and data validation.

milestones.label: M03
milestones.o: Rajesh can build backend services with an understanding of performance, basic scalability, and effective logging/monitoring, preparing for high-load environments.
milestones.module_count_rationale: 4 modules are necessary to cover performance optimization, asynchronous programming, logging/monitoring, and message queues, which are foundational for building scalable and observable services.
milestones.identity_statement: You are becoming a Scalable Services Intern, building robust backends that can handle increasing user demands.

project.title: Optimized Content Delivery Service
project.description: Optimize a backend service for content delivery (e.g., images, articles) to improve response times. Implement basic caching, comprehensive logging, and integrate a monitoring solution.
project.deliverable: A deployed, optimized service with performance metrics (e.g., response time, throughput) demonstrating improvements, detailed logs, and a dashboard showing key metrics.
seeded_errors: The caching layer is incorrectly configured, leading to stale data being served to users, requiring the learner to implement cache invalidation strategies.

modules.title: Performance Optimization & Caching
modules.skill_count_rationale: 5 skills are crucial for identifying and resolving performance bottlenecks, and leveraging caching to improve response times and reduce load.

skills.title: Backend Performance Profiling
skills.why_this_skill: Profiling helps identify bottlenecks in your code, allowing you to optimize critical paths and improve overall application responsiveness.

scenario.title: Diagnosing a Latency Spike in Production

skills.title: Database Performance Optimization
skills.why_this_skill: Optimizing database interactions is often the most impactful way to improve backend performance, as data access is a common bottleneck.

scenario.title: Optimizing a Complex Join Query

skills.title: Caching Strategies & Implementation
skills.why_this_skill: Caching is a powerful technique to reduce database load and improve API response times by storing frequently accessed data closer to the application.

scenario.title: Implementing a Cache for Frequently Accessed Data

skills.title: Introduction to Load Balancing
skills.why_this_skill: Load balancing distributes incoming traffic across multiple servers, improving application availability and scalability.

scenario.title: Configuring a Load Balancer for a Web Service

skills.title: Content Delivery Networks (CDNs)
skills.why_this_skill: CDNs significantly improve the delivery speed of static assets (images, videos) by caching them closer to users, reducing latency and server load.
lessons: Integrating a CDN with a Backend

scenario.title: Speeding Up Image Delivery with a CDN

modules.title: Asynchronous Programming & Concurrency
modules.skill_count_rationale: 5 skills are essential for building responsive and efficient backend services by handling multiple operations concurrently without blocking.

skills.title: Asynchronous Programming with Async/Await
skills.why_this_skill: Async/await is a modern pattern for writing non-blocking code, crucial for high-performance I/O-bound operations in backend services.

scenario.title: Refactoring a Blocking I/O Operation

skills.title: Concurrency & Parallelism Concepts
skills.why_this_skill: Understanding concurrency and parallelism is vital for designing systems that can handle multiple tasks simultaneously, improving throughput and responsiveness.

scenario.title: Designing a Concurrent Task Executor

skills.title: Thread Safety & Race Conditions
skills.why_this_skill: Ensuring thread safety is crucial for preventing data corruption and unexpected behavior in concurrent applications.

scenario.title: Debugging a Concurrency Bug in a Shared Resource

skills.title: Futures, Promises & Callbacks
skills.why_this_skill: These patterns are fundamental for managing the results of asynchronous operations and orchestrating complex workflows.

scenario.title: Orchestrating Multiple Asynchronous API Calls

skills.title: Event-Driven Architecture Basics
skills.why_this_skill: Event-driven architectures enable highly scalable and decoupled systems, crucial for modern distributed applications.

scenario.title: Designing a Simple Event-Driven Microservice

science.desc: A backend service is experiencing high latency due to blocking I/O operations. Refactor a critical path to use asynchronous programming to improve responsiveness.

modules.title: Logging, Monitoring & Observability
modules.skill_count_rationale: 5 skills are vital for understanding the health and performance of backend systems, enabling quick issue detection and resolution.

skills.title: Structured Logging Best Practices
skills.why_this_skill: Structured logs are machine-readable and easily searchable, making it much faster to diagnose issues in complex systems.

scenario.title: Implementing Structured Logging for a New Service

skills.title: Centralized Logging Systems (Concepts)
skills.why_this_skill: Centralized logging aggregates logs from all services, providing a single pane of glass for monitoring and troubleshooting distributed systems.

scenario.title: Searching for an Error Across Multiple Services

skills.title: Metrics & Monitoring Fundamentals
skills.why_this_skill: Monitoring key metrics provides real-time insights into application health and performance, enabling proactive issue detection.

scenario.title: Setting Up Basic API Latency Monitoring

skills.title: Alerting & Basic Incident Response
skills.why_this_skill: Effective alerting ensures that critical issues are detected and communicated promptly, enabling quick incident response and minimizing downtime.

scenario.title: Responding to a Critical Service Downtime Alert

skills.title: Introduction to Distributed Tracing
skills.why_this_skill: Distributed tracing helps visualize requests as they flow through multiple services, invaluable for debugging complex microservices architectures.

scenario.title: Tracing a Request Across Multiple API Calls

modules.title: Introduction to Message Queues
modules.skill_count_rationale: 5 skills are crucial for understanding how to decouple services, handle background tasks, and build resilient, scalable distributed systems.

skills.title: Message Queue Fundamentals
skills.why_this_skill: Message queues are essential for decoupling services, handling asynchronous tasks, and building scalable, fault-tolerant architectures.

scenario.title: Designing an Asynchronous Email Notification System

skills.title: Implementing Background Tasks
skills.why_this_skill: Offloading long-running operations to background tasks improves API responsiveness and user experience.

scenario.title: Processing Large Data Imports in the Background

skills.title: Publish-Subscribe (Pub/Sub) Patterns
skills.why_this_skill: Pub/Sub enables flexible, scalable communication between services without direct dependencies, ideal for event-driven architectures.

scenario.title: Broadcasting Updates to Multiple Services

skills.title: Idempotent Operations & Message Delivery
skills.why_this_skill: Designing idempotent operations is crucial for reliable message processing in distributed systems, preventing unintended side effects from retries.

scenario.title: Handling Duplicate Payment Notifications

skills.title: Dead-Letter Queues (DLQs)
skills.why_this_skill: DLQs are crucial for handling message processing failures gracefully, preventing data loss and enabling later analysis of problematic messages.

scenario.title: Handling Unprocessable Messages in a Queue

science.desc: Explain how you would use caching and message queues to improve the scalability and responsiveness of a backend service that processes user-generated content.

milestones.label: M04
milestones.o: Rajesh can contribute to microservices architectures, understand distributed systems challenges, and deploy services reliably, preparing for junior backend roles.
milestones.module_count_rationale: 4 modules are essential to cover microservices architecture, inter-service communication, distributed data management, and advanced deployment, which are key for junior backend engineers in modern distributed systems.
milestones.identity_statement: You are becoming a Junior Backend Engineer, building and integrating services in complex distributed environments.

project.title: Microservice for User Notifications
project.description: Design and implement a new microservice responsible for sending user notifications (email, SMS). This service should integrate with existing user data and communicate asynchronously with other services.
project.deliverable: A deployed microservice with its own API, database (if needed), and integration with a message queue. Include a design document outlining its architecture and communication patterns.
seeded_errors: The notification microservice has a tight coupling with the user service's database, requiring the learner to refactor for loose coupling via API calls or events.

modules.title: Microservices Architecture
modules.skill_count_rationale: 5 skills are crucial for understanding the principles, benefits, and challenges of designing and working with microservices architectures.

skills.title: Microservices Principles & Benefits
skills.why_this_skill: Microservices are a dominant architectural style; understanding their principles is key for modern backend development and scalability.

scenario.title: Deciding to Break Down a Monolith

skills.title: Service Discovery & Registration
skills.why_this_skill: Service discovery allows microservices to find and communicate with each other dynamically, essential in elastic, distributed environments.

scenario.title: Implementing Service Discovery for a New Microservice

skills.title: API Composition & Aggregation
skills.why_this_skill: API composition patterns help build user-facing APIs by aggregating data from multiple microservices, simplifying client-side development.

scenario.title: Designing an API for a Mobile Client

skills.title: Domain-Driven Design (DDD) Basics
skills.why_this_skill: DDD helps align software design with business domain logic, leading to more maintainable and understandable microservices.

scenario.title: Defining Bounded Contexts for a New Feature

skills.title: Saga Pattern for Distributed Transactions (Concepts)
skills.why_this_skill: The Saga pattern addresses the challenge of maintaining data consistency across multiple microservices without traditional distributed transactions.

scenario.title: Designing a Distributed Order Fulfillment Process

modules.title: Inter-Service Communication
modules.skill_count_rationale: 5 skills are critical for enabling efficient and reliable communication between different microservices in a distributed system.

skills.title: REST vs. RPC for Inter-Service Communication
skills.why_this_skill: Choosing the right communication protocol is vital for microservices; understanding REST and RPC trade-offs is key.

video.title: REST vs. RPC for Inter-Service Communication

scenario.title: Choosing Communication for a Real-time Service

skills.title: Event Streaming Basics (Kafka/RabbitMQ concepts)
skills.why_this_skill: Event streaming platforms enable real-time data pipelines and highly scalable, decoupled microservices communication.

scenario.title: Designing a Real-time Data Pipeline

skills.title: Circuit Breaker Pattern
skills.why_this_skill: The Circuit Breaker pattern prevents cascading failures in distributed systems, improving resilience and fault tolerance.

scenario.title: Preventing Cascading Failures in a Microservice

skills.title: Retry & Timeout Patterns
skills.why_this_skill: Implementing robust retry and timeout mechanisms is essential for handling transient failures and preventing indefinite blocking in distributed systems.

scenario.title: Handling Transient Network Failures

skills.title: API Contract Management
skills.why_this_skill: Managing API contracts ensures consistency and compatibility between services, preventing integration issues as APIs evolve.

scenario.title: Ensuring Compatibility Between Microservices

science.desc: A critical microservice is failing intermittently due to dependencies on other services. Implement a circuit breaker pattern to prevent cascading failures and improve resilience.

modules.title: Distributed Data Management
modules.skill_count_rationale: 5 skills are crucial for understanding how to manage data across multiple services, ensuring consistency and availability in distributed environments.

skills.title: Data Consistency Models (CAP Theorem)
skills.why_this_skill: Understanding consistency models and the CAP theorem is fundamental for designing resilient and scalable distributed databases.

scenario.title: Choosing a Consistency Model for a User Profile Service

skills.title: Database Sharding & Partitioning
skills.why_this_skill: Sharding is a key technique for scaling databases horizontally, distributing data across multiple servers to handle high loads.

scenario.title: Designing a Sharding Strategy for a Large E-commerce Database

skills.title: Data Replication & High Availability
skills.why_this_skill: Replication ensures data availability and fault tolerance, critical for preventing data loss and minimizing downtime in production systems.

scenario.title: Ensuring Database High Availability

skills.title: Distributed Caching Systems (Redis/Memcached concepts)
skills.why_this_skill: Distributed caches are vital for scaling high-traffic applications, providing fast data access across multiple service instances.

scenario.title: Implementing a Distributed Cache for User Sessions

skills.title: Data Migration Strategies
skills.why_this_skill: Safely moving and transforming data is a common task in evolving systems; understanding migration strategies is crucial for minimizing downtime and data loss.

scenario.title: Performing a Zero-Downtime Database Schema Change

modules.title: Advanced Deployment & CI/CD Basics
modules.skill_count_rationale: 5 skills are crucial for automating the software delivery pipeline, ensuring rapid, reliable, and consistent deployments of backend services.

skills.title: CI/CD Pipeline Fundamentals
skills.why_this_skill: CI/CD automates the build, test, and deployment process, enabling faster and more reliable software delivery, a core DevOps practice.

scenario.title: Automating Code Builds and Tests

skills.title: Container Orchestration Basics (Kubernetes Concepts)
skills.why_this_skill: Kubernetes is the de facto standard for container orchestration, essential for deploying, scaling, and managing containerized applications in production.

scenario.title: Deploying a Containerized Application to Kubernetes

skills.title: Blue/Green & Canary Deployments (Concepts)
skills.why_this_skill: Advanced deployment strategies minimize downtime and risk, allowing for seamless updates and quick rollbacks in production.

scenario.title: Planning a Zero-Downtime Feature Rollout

skills.title: Infrastructure as Code (IaC) Basics (Terraform Concepts)
skills.why_this_skill: IaC allows you to manage and provision infrastructure through code, ensuring consistency, repeatability, and version control for your cloud resources.

scenario.title: Provisioning a Cloud Database with IaC

skills.title: Serverless Functions (AWS Lambda/Azure Functions Concepts)
skills.why_this_skill: Serverless functions offer a cost-effective and scalable way to run backend code without managing servers, ideal for event-driven microservices.

scenario.title: Implementing a Serverless API Endpoint

science.desc: You're tasked with deploying a new microservice. Describe the CI/CD pipeline you would set up and the deployment strategy you would choose to minimize risk.

milestones.label: M05
milestones.o: Rajesh can build, deploy, and maintain robust backend systems, troubleshoot production issues, and ensure system reliability and security.
milestones.module_count_rationale: 4 modules are critical to cover fault tolerance, advanced testing, incident response, and infrastructure as code, which are essential for building and maintaining highly reliable production systems.
milestones.identity_statement: You are becoming a Production-Ready Backend Engineer, building and maintaining highly reliable systems that stand the test of real-world usage.

project.title: Fault-Tolerant Data Processing Pipeline
project.description: Implement a fault-tolerant data processing pipeline for a real-time analytics dashboard. This project should demonstrate resilience against failures, robust error handling, and effective monitoring.
project.deliverable: A deployed data pipeline that can recover from transient failures, processes data reliably, and includes a dashboard showing its health and throughput.
seeded_errors: A critical component in the pipeline fails silently, causing data loss without alerting. The learner must implement robust error handling and alerting for all stages.

modules.title: Fault Tolerance & Resilience
modules.skill_count_rationale: 5 skills are crucial for designing and implementing systems that can withstand failures and continue operating reliably in production environments.

skills.title: Resilience Patterns for Distributed Systems
skills.why_this_skill: Building resilient systems is paramount to ensure continuous availability and graceful degradation in the face of failures.

scenario.title: Designing a Resilient Payment Gateway

skills.title: Introduction to Chaos Engineering
skills.why_this_skill: Chaos engineering proactively identifies weaknesses in systems by injecting failures, improving resilience before they impact users.

scenario.title: Planning a Chaos Experiment for a Microservice

skills.title: Disaster Recovery & Backup Strategies
skills.why_this_skill: Disaster recovery planning is essential for minimizing data loss and downtime in the event of catastrophic failures.

scenario.title: Designing a Disaster Recovery Plan for a Database

skills.title: Graceful Degradation & Feature Toggles
skills.why_this_skill: Graceful degradation ensures that core functionality remains available even when parts of the system fail, improving user experience during outages.

scenario.title: Implementing a Fallback for a Third-Party API

skills.title: Security Auditing & Vulnerability Scanning (Concepts)
skills.why_this_skill: Regular security auditing and scanning are crucial for identifying and mitigating vulnerabilities before they can be exploited.

scenario.title: Responding to a Detected Security Vulnerability

modules.title: Advanced Testing Strategies
modules.skill_count_rationale: 5 skills are crucial for ensuring the quality, reliability, and correctness of complex backend systems through comprehensive testing approaches.

skills.title: Test-Driven Development (TDD)
skills.why_this_skill: TDD is a development practice that improves code quality, reduces bugs, and ensures that code is testable by design.

scenario.title: Developing a New Feature Using TDD

skills.title: End-to-End (E2E) Testing
skills.why_this_skill: E2E tests simulate real user scenarios, verifying that the entire application stack works correctly from frontend to backend.

scenario.title: Automating a User Workflow Test

skills.title: Performance & Load Testing
skills.why_this_skill: Performance and load testing ensure that backend services can handle expected (and unexpected) traffic, preventing outages under stress.

scenario.title: Identifying Performance Bottlenecks Under Load

skills.title: Contract Testing for Microservices
skills.why_this_skill: Contract testing ensures that microservices can communicate correctly without needing full integration tests, speeding up development and reducing coupling.

scenario.title: Ensuring API Compatibility with Contract Tests

skills.title: Security Testing (SAST/DAST Concepts)
skills.why_this_skill: Integrating security testing into the development lifecycle helps catch vulnerabilities early, reducing the risk of security breaches.

scenario.title: Automating Security Scans in CI/CD

science.desc: A critical backend service is experiencing intermittent failures under high load, but unit tests pass. Design and execute a load test to identify the breaking point and propose solutions.

modules.title: Incident Response & Post-Mortems
modules.skill_count_rationale: 5 skills are crucial for effectively responding to production incidents, minimizing impact, and learning from failures to prevent recurrence.

skills.title: Incident Management Process
skills.why_this_skill: A well-defined incident management process ensures a structured and efficient response to production issues, minimizing downtime and impact.

scenario.title: Leading a Critical Incident Response

skills.title: Root Cause Analysis (RCA) Techniques
skills.why_this_skill: RCA helps identify the underlying causes of incidents, preventing recurrence and improving system reliability.

scenario.title: Conducting an RCA for a Database Outage

skills.title: Blameless Post-Mortem Culture
skills.why_this_skill: A blameless post-mortem culture fosters learning from failures, improving system reliability and team collaboration without fear of punishment.

scenario.title: Facilitating a Blameless Post-Mortem Meeting

skills.title: SLAs, SLOs, & SLIs (Concepts)
skills.why_this_skill: Defining and monitoring Service Level Objectives (SLOs) is crucial for managing expectations, ensuring service reliability, and driving operational improvements.

scenario.title: Defining SLOs for a Critical API Service

skills.title: Runbooks & Playbooks for Operations
skills.why_this_skill: Well-documented runbooks and playbooks enable consistent and efficient responses to common operational issues, reducing cognitive load during incidents.

scenario.title: Creating a Runbook for Database Connection Issues

modules.title: Advanced Infrastructure as Code
modules.skill_count_rationale: 5 skills are crucial for managing complex cloud infrastructure programmatically, ensuring scalability, consistency, and cost-effectiveness.

skills.title: Cloud Provider Ecosystems (AWS/Azure/GCP Concepts)
skills.why_this_skill: Understanding major cloud providers' services is essential for designing and deploying scalable and cost-effective backend solutions.
lessons: Azure Core Services (VMs, Storage, SQL DB)
GCP Core Services (Compute Engine, Cloud Storage)

scenario.title: Choosing Cloud Services for a New Application

skills.title: Advanced Terraform for Cloud Infrastructure
skills.why_this_skill: Mastering Terraform allows for robust, scalable, and version-controlled management of complex cloud infrastructure across providers.

scenario.title: Managing Multi-Environment Deployments with Terraform

skills.title: Advanced Kubernetes Concepts
skills.why_this_skill: Deep understanding of Kubernetes is essential for managing and scaling containerized microservices in production environments.

scenario.title: Scaling a Microservice with Kubernetes HPA

skills.title: Secrets Management in Production
skills.why_this_skill: Securely managing sensitive data like API keys and database credentials is a critical security practice for production applications.

scenario.title: Implementing Secure API Key Rotation

skills.title: Secrets Management in Production Advanced
skills.why_this_skill: Securely managing sensitive data like API keys and database credentials is a critical security practice for production applications.

scenario.title: Implementing Secure API Key Rotation
